channel.title: EMERGENZ Biosecurity Intelligence Dashboard
channel.description: High-severity biosecurity signals and recent authority/media coverage. Generated 2026-06-04T10:06:43.744Z.

item.title: [ACTION] Ebola disease — Bundibugyo virus, Democratic Republic of the Congo
item.description: WHO has declared the Bundibugyo ebolavirus outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, with cross-border spread to Uganda. Per the WHO emergency situation page (data as of 31 May 2026 for the DRC and 1 June 2026 for Uganda), the DRC has reported 321 confirmed cases, 48 confirmed deaths (CFR 15%), and 116 suspected cases, and Uganda has reported 9 confirmed cases (1 death) — 330 confirmed cases and 49 deaths in total. Confirmed cases have risen sharply since the late-May WHO DON605 snapshot (125 confirmed) as laboratory testing reclassified earlier suspected cases, so the suspected count has correspondingly fallen from about 906 to 116. The regional response has escalated: Uganda border health measures, a contested US-supported quarantine/care arrangement in Kenya, a Brazilian investigation of possible imported cases, and accelerated multi-candidate vaccine efforts. WHO updates these figures daily from DRC government (COUSP-DRC) situation reports and they are subject to retrospective revision — verify the latest WHO situation report before operational use.

item.title: [CONCERN] Andes virus hantavirus cluster — MV Hondius travel-linked
item.description: Andes hantavirus cluster linked to a South America–Antarctica expedition cruise aboard MV Hondius. 13 reported cases (11 confirmed, 2 probable) and 3 deaths across multiple countries, with multinational case-finding and post-exposure monitoring continuing.

item.title: [CONCERN] Measles — United States
item.description: CDC confirmed 1,983 measles cases across 40 jurisdictions in 2026 (as of May 28), representing 30 outbreaks with 93% of cases outbreak-associated. No deaths reported in 2026. U.S. kindergartener MMR coverage has declined to 92.5% (from 95.2% in 2019–2020), leaving approximately 286,000 kindergarteners unvaccinated — the structural driver of sustained outbreak activity.

item.title: [CONCERN] Mpox — clade I, Africa & travel-linked importations
item.description: Mpox clade I and travel-linked importation monitoring remain operationally significant for clinical recognition, specimen routing, and laboratory clade differentiation. Verify current country-level trends and any importation details against specific Africa CDC, WHO, or CDC updates before publication.

item.title: [CONCERN] Cholera — sub-Saharan Africa, including Republic of Congo
item.description: Africa CDC reported 40,707 cholera cases continent-wide from January 1 through May 14, 2026 — a 65% year-over-year decline compared to the same period in 2025. Despite the overall volume decrease, case fatality ratios remain elevated in conflict-affected and low-healthcare-access settings. Sudan, Ethiopia, Somalia, Mozambique, Malawi, and Zimbabwe continue reporting active transmission.

item.title: [CONCERN] Chikungunya — global outbreak activity
item.description: Chikungunya surveillance remains active across parts of the Americas and other mosquito-active regions. Verify current country burden and case totals against specific PAHO or WHO updates before publication.

item.title: Google News: Opinion | Ebola is spreading in ways no one fully grasps. Here’s how to stop it. - The Washington Post
item.description: Opinion | Ebola is spreading in ways no one fully grasps. Here’s how to stop it. The Washington Post

item.title: Google News: European approval enables measles protection with CSL’s immunoglobulin Privigen - PharmaTimes
item.description: European approval enables measles protection with CSL’s immunoglobulin Privigen PharmaTimes

item.title: Google News: CT epidemiologists respond to measles outbreaks, plus a look at Ebola - Connecticut Public
item.description: CT epidemiologists respond to measles outbreaks, plus a look at Ebola Connecticut Public

item.title: UKHSA: Guidance: Adolescent vaccination programme in secondary schools for 2026 to 2027
item.description: Information on the adolescent vaccination programme delivered in secondary schools and the role that schools play.

item.title: UKHSA: Guidance: Flu vaccination in schools
item.description: Flu vaccination guidance and resources for schools.

item.title: NBC News: Flesh-eating screwworm returns to U.S. after 60 years, threatening cattle herd
item.description: A flesh-eating parasite that had been kept out of U.S. livestock for decades has been detected in Texas, threatening the nation’s cattle industry and food supply at a time when prices are already high.

item.title: Google News: Norovirus Not a Winter Virus - Cleaning & Maintenance Management
item.description: Norovirus Not a Winter Virus Cleaning & Maintenance Management

item.title: ABC News: Melinda French Gates donates $215 million to improve women's health worldwide
item.description: Melinda French Gates is expanding her efforts to improve women's health worldwide, pledging $215 million for contraceptive access and maternal care, as well as initiatives for middle-age women

item.title: Google News: Florida Commissioner of Agriculture Issues Emergency Rule to Protect Against HPAI - Southeast AgNET
item.description: Florida Commissioner of Agriculture Issues Emergency Rule to Protect Against HPAI Southeast AgNET

item.title: Google News: The Ebola Outbreak and the Need for Stronger Global Health Governance - Middle East Council on Global Affairs
item.description: The Ebola Outbreak and the Need for Stronger Global Health Governance Middle East Council on Global Affairs

item.title: Google News: Priest’s Ebola Death Stirs Fear an Doubt in Congolese Town - The New York Times
item.description: Priest’s Ebola Death Stirs Fear an Doubt in Congolese Town The New York Times

item.title: NPR: Some students with disabilities rely on screens at school. What happens if they're banned?
item.description: Some students with disabilities rely on assistive technology to learn, and they worry it could be swept up in the movement to get screens out of schools.

item.title: Google News: 2 scientists charged with bringing deactivated mpox virus into the US and lying to authorities - Bedford Gazette
item.description: 2 scientists charged with bringing deactivated mpox virus into the US and lying to authorities Bedford Gazette

item.title: Google News: Congo Eurobonds Defy Ebola as Investors Chase Rare Yield, AI Exposure - Bloomberg.com
item.description: Congo Eurobonds Defy Ebola as Investors Chase Rare Yield, AI Exposure Bloomberg.com

item.title: NPR: With World Cup set to begin, public health officials hope to defend against germs
item.description: The World Cup gets underway next week and millions will pack huge stadiums to watch. Public health officials will be watching too, only they're looking for germs that could spread at these matches!

item.title: NPR: Weakened public health powers could hamper states' outbreak responses
item.description: Public health experts are worried that states are less prepared to respond to the Ebola outbreak and other infectious disease threats because many weakened their public health authorities in the wake of the COVID-19 pandemic.

item.title: Google News: Rebel attacks in eastern DRC kill 30 people and hamper Ebola response - The Guardian
item.description: Rebel attacks in eastern DRC kill 30 people and hamper Ebola response The Guardian

item.title: UKHSA: Research: Health Protection Report volume 20 (2026)
item.description: A national public health bulletin from the UK Health Security Agency (UKHSA).

item.title: UKHSA: Research: STI quarterly surveillance reports: provisional data
item.description: The latest quarterly reports of provisional data for syphilis and gonorrhoea diagnoses in England.

item.title: UKHSA: Official Statistics: Respiratory syncytial virus (RSV) maternal vaccination coverage in England
item.description: Official statistics data and reporting on vaccine uptake for the RSV (respiratory syncytial virus) vaccination programme for pregnant women.
